name: agents-system
description: npm-shipped default plugins for agents-cli — the lightweight, no-paid-key workflows that ship with the system layer. Heavier or key-gated plugins live in .agents-extras instead.

plugins.name: clify
plugins.description: clify â€” turn any web service into an installable, verified CLI for humans and agents. `/clify <target>` climbs a reverse-engineering ladder (docs/OpenAPI â†’ authed API â†’ HAR capture â†’ content-script injection), infers a typed command schema, and emits a globally-installable CLI â€” optionally an MCP server via `--mcp`. No command ships until it has hit the real endpoint and returned real output (the verification gate). Builds on `agents browser` (capture), `agents secrets` (Keychain token persistence), `mcporter`/`agents mcp` (the `--mcp` emit), and `oss` (open-sourcing a generated CLI).

plugins.name: cloud
plugins.description: cloud - Rush Cloud dispatch plugin. `/cloud:run` documents the native `rush cloud run` path for Claude Code/Codex harnesses, repo dispatch, execution lifecycle commands, and verification evidence. `/cloud:accounts` covers Rush login plus connected Claude/Codex credentials (`rush cloud accounts add/list/remove`) and distinguishes Rush Cloud access/subscription gates from vendor account capacity.

plugins.name: code
plugins.description: Coding-workflow plugin for the manager/router engineering loop. `/code:loop` drains a queue end-to-end (single ticket or many: plan, code, test, review, rebase, fix CI, merge), `/code:dispatch` triages a single task and picks the delivery path, `/code:verify` runs the end-to-end gate (canonical test per changed surface, read from the project's CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md), `/code:review` reviews every PR opened in parallel — plus a security pass on risk-touching diffs — and merges per verdict, `/code:ship` is the post-merge gate for distributables (publish, confirm live on the public channel, activate where it runs, verify the real surface), `/code:sprint` runs a time-boxed multi-track parallel push via `agents teams`, `/code:quality` runs a read-only code-health diagnostic and opens an HTML report, `/code:learn` reflects on a session and routes durable lessons to the right skill/rule/memory without overfitting, `/commit` splits the working tree into the maximum number of small logical commits.

plugins.name: fleet
plugins.description: fleet â€” fleet-wide operations across every machine registered with `agents devices`. `/fleet:sync` pulls every registered DotAgent repo (system, user, and extras) to origin latest on every online device, then refreshes the installed agents â€” non-destructive (git merge --ff-only only; reports repos blocked by local edits instead of clobbering), handling the fast-forward workaround, the transient GitHub-SSH throttle, and Windows PowerShell quoting, ending with a repo x device matrix. `/fleet:onboard <device>` brings a bare new device up to fleet parity (agents-cli, agent CLIs, DotAgent repos, shared SSH key, PATH shim, device registration, agent auth) â€” discovery-first, additive, idempotent, credentials via sanctioned paths only, never copied host-to-host.

plugins.name: git
plugins.description: git — pure git plumbing commands that aren't tied to code logic. `/git:prune` removes merged branches and worktrees with hard data-loss guards (skips anything with uncommitted changes, stashes, unmerged commits, locks, or detached HEAD), using `git rev-list --count origin/$MAIN..HEAD == 0` as the load-bearing 'nothing to lose' check. `/git:tag-release` creates and pushes an annotated release tag (never force, never re-points an existing tag). The code-aware loop (`/commit`, `/review`, `/sprint`) lives in the `code` plugin.

plugins.name: share
plugins.description: share â€” publish an HTML artifact (a plan, viz, or report) to a shareable link on your own Cloudflare R2 (zero egress, ~$0) via `agents share`. `/share:public` posts a public link with an auto-generated OG cover (a screenshot of the page's hero) so it unfurls into a preview card in Slack/iMessage/Twitter/Discord; `/share:private` posts an unlisted, auto-expiring link with no preview card (unguessable but still public-read â€” not authenticated). Requires a one-time `agents share setup` (provisions an R2 bucket + Worker) or `agents share join`.

plugins.name: social
plugins.description: social — turn a content agent's post archive into strategy. social:audit (parse+dedup+cluster+12x3 taxonomy), social:align (audience/ICP fit: buyer vs amplifier, gaps + re-weighting), social:schedule (coverage-deduped engagement-rubric backlog scheduled to X/LinkedIn via getlate). Built from a real ~4,600-draft audit.

plugins.name: swarm
plugins.description: swarm — fan a task out across a team of parallel coding agents via `agents teams` (the modern replacement for the deprecated Swarmify MCP). `/swarm:run` is the generic fan-out — decompose an arbitrary goal into independent tracks, spawn a mixed team, monitor, and synthesize (the catch-all for work that doesn't fit the specialized modes); `/swarm:plan` researches hard and drafts an OpenSpec-grade change proposal, then has independent agents plan it blind and reconciles; `/swarm:spec` reverse-engineers the OpenSpec source-of-truth spec (requirements + Given/When/Then scenarios) from the real code, then has independent agents spec it blind and drift-checks every requirement against actual behavior; `/swarm:debug` traces the data path and confirms the root cause with blind, different-provider verifiers (scaled to the bug) before any fix; `/swarm:test` splits a scope into areas and covers each in parallel; `/swarm:qa` maps an app's QA surface, provisions instances on multiple ports, and fans QA agents across them in waves via `agents browser`, then ranks a ship/no-ship verdict. Each command invokes its same-named skill; the shared `agents teams` engine (provider discovery, boundary contracts, judgment-based sizing) lives in the internal `swarm:orchestrate` skill.
